perturbation.original_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information. Upon the termination by ei ther party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same. Upon request, the fact of any such destruction must be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by an officer of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information. Upon the termination by either party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes but will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all other Confidential Information, including all copies of the same. Upon request, the fact of any such destruction must be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by an officer of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party must return or destroy ALL Confidential Information but then stating that one copy can be retained for archival purposes. This introduces ambiguity about what is required upon termination or request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information. Upon the termination by either party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes but will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all other Confidential Information, including all copies of the same. Upon request, the fact of any such destruction must be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by an officer of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination or request, the Receiving Party will return or destroy ALL Confidential Information. The changed text modifies this by stating that the Receiving Party will retain one copy of all Confidential Information for archival purposes but will return or destroy all *other* Confidential Information. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, confirming that the changed text is indeed present in another part of the document. This creates a clear contradiction because the instruction to return or destroy 'all' is directly contradicted by the permission to retain one copy, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Compelled Disclosure. If either party here to, as Receiving Party, is requested or required by legal or administrative process to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, such Receiving Party will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement so that such Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or other relief. In any case, such Receiving Party will (a) disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party’s legal counsel advises is required to be disclosed, (b) use be st efforts to ensure that such Confidential Information is treated confidentially, and (c) notify such Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably practicable of the items of Confidential Information so disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Compelled Disclosure. If either party hereto, as Receiving Party, is requested or required by legal or administrative process to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement. In any case, such Receiving Party will (a) disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party’s legal counsel advises is required to be disclosed, (b) use best efforts to ensure that such Confidential Information is treated confidentially, and (c) notify such Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably practicable of the items of Confidential Information so disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence states the Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party, but then clause (c) requires the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party 'as soon as reasonably practicable'. This leads to a conflicting requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Compelled Disclosure. If either party hereto, as Receiving Party, is requested or required by legal or administrative process to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement. In any case, such Receiving Party will (a) disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party’s legal counsel advises is required to be disclosed, (b) use best efforts to ensure that such Confidential Information is treated confidentially, and (c) notify such Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably practicable of the items of Confidential Information so disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 initially states that the Receiving Party is 'not required to notify' the Disclosing Party of a request to disclose confidential information. However, clause (c) of the same section then mandates that the Receiving Party 'notify such Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably practicable of the items of Confidential Information so disclosed.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section of the contract, making it an in-text contradiction as described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, because there are conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to t he parties hereto, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favor of the other without proof of irreparable harm or the posting of any bond. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, the primarily prevailing party will be entitled to recover its costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the parties hereto, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favor of the other without proof of irreparable harm or the posting of any bond. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, the primarily prevailing party will not be entitled to recover its costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction regarding attorney's fees. It initially implies the availability of injunctive relief as a remedy, but then contradicts the standard litigation clause by stating the prevailing party will NOT recover costs and fees, which is contradictory to normal litigation practices.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the parties hereto, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favor of the other without proof of irreparable harm or the posting of any bond. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, the primarily prevailing party will not be entitled to recover its costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7, stating that in the event of litigation, the prevailing party 'will not be entitled to recover its costs and expenses,' including attorney's fees. However, the contradicted text, which is at the same location, also states that the prevailing party 'will not be entitled to recover its costs and expenses.' This creates a direct conflict within Section 7 itself. The change alters the original clause, causing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall govern all communications of Confidential Information between the parties until terminated by either party; provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure with respect to Confidential Information shall continue beyond termination until: (a) such time as the Confidential Information is no {J1872881.2} 3 longer deemed to be Confidential Information as more fully set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement or (b) upon the written consent of the Disclosing Party that the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure have ended.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall govern all communications of Confidential Information between the parties until terminated by either party; provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure with respect to Confidential Information shall terminate immediately upon termination of this agreement. (a) such time as the Confidential Information is no {J1872881.2} 3 longer deemed to be Confidential Information as more fully set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement or (b) upon the written consent of the Disclosing Party that the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure have ended.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction: The initial sentence states the confidentiality obligations continue BEYOND termination under certain conditions, and then immediately says that confidentiality obligation terminates upon the termination of this agreement, causing in-text contradiction. This introduces uncertainty and the end of the confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall govern all communications of Confidential Information between the parties until terminated by either party; provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure with respect to Confidential Information shall terminate immediately upon termination of this agreement. (a) such time as the Confidential Information is no {J1872881.2} 3 longer deemed to be Confidential Information as more fully set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement or (b) upon the written consent of the Disclosing Party that the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure have ended.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations continue beyond termination under certain conditions. The changed text, however, states that confidentiality obligations terminate immediately upon termination of the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction within section 9 itself. The contradiction is strong because it creates immediate uncertainty regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. The changed text, as highlighted in the explanation, creates an in-text contradiction as specified in the definition for "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction".
